situation: What does your current production planning process look like?
How do you track quality metrics across your production lines?
What systems do you use for inventory management and supply chain visibility?
How many suppliers do you work with, and how do you manage those relationships?
What does your maintenance schedule look like — reactive, preventive, or predictive?
How do you handle compliance documentation and audit trails?
What's your current OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) tracking setup?
How do shop floor operators communicate issues to management today?
What does your quality control process look like from raw material to shipped product?
How do you manage production scheduling when demand fluctuates?
problem: What's your biggest challenge with unplanned downtime?
Where do quality issues most commonly surface — and how long does it take to catch them?
What's the most frustrating part of managing your supply chain?
How much scrap or rework does your current process generate?
What happens when a key machine goes down unexpectedly?
How difficult is it to trace the root cause of a quality issue?
Where does paperwork or manual data entry slow your operations down?
What's your biggest challenge with workforce scheduling and skill matching?
How often do production plans need to be changed at the last minute?
What visibility do you have into real-time production status?
How challenging is it to maintain consistent quality across shifts?
What's the biggest bottleneck in your order-to-delivery timeline?
How do you handle it when customer specifications change mid-production?
What's the hardest part of staying compliant with [ISO/industry standard]?
How much time do your engineers spend firefighting vs. improving processes?
implication: If unplanned downtime continues at current levels, what does that cost you annually?
When a quality issue makes it to a customer, what's the real impact beyond the replacement cost?
How does [supply chain visibility gap] affect your ability to make delivery commitments?
If scrap rates stay where they are, what does that do to your margins over 12 months?
What's at stake if you miss an audit finding and your certification is at risk?
When firefighting takes up engineering time, what improvement projects don't get done?
How does [problem] affect your ability to win new contracts with quality-sensitive customers?
If production planning can't respond to demand changes quickly, what happens to customer satisfaction?
What's the compound cost of manual data entry errors across your entire operation?
How does [maintenance issue] affect your equipment lifespan and capex planning?
If you can't trace root causes quickly, how many repeat issues go unresolved?
What happens to worker safety when [problem] creates shortcuts or workarounds?
How does inconsistent shift quality affect your ability to scale to a second or third shift?
If [bottleneck] limits your throughput, how much revenue capacity are you leaving unused?
What's the risk to your reputation if [quality issue] reaches a major customer?
need_payoff: If you could predict machine failures 48 hours before they happen, what would that save you?
How would it change your margins if scrap was cut by 30%?
What would it be worth to have real-time visibility into every production line from your office?
If audit prep took 2 days instead of 2 weeks, what would your team do with that time?
How would it impact customer relationships if your on-time delivery rate was 98%+?
What would it mean for your business if quality was consistent across all shifts, all lines?
If engineers could spend 80% of their time on improvement instead of firefighting, what would change?
How much would it be worth to cut your order-to-delivery time by a week?
If you could trace any quality issue to its root cause in minutes, how would that change your response?
What would a 5% improvement in OEE mean for your annual output and revenue?
